channel.title: Comments for page "I've Been Through the Desert on a Horse with No Name"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "I've Been Through the Desert on a Horse with No Name"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not going to lie, the part where the writer Nelson seemingly becoming something akin to salt reminded me of something.

It seemed like it came out of a story from H.P Lovecraft, not that it's a bad thing mind you, but it feels similar to the Dunwich Horror or Colors Out of Space (if I ever get to reading that story.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They work fine for me? I'll have some other people test them out, but when I was writing the thing, it was the other way around: I accidentally did http instead of https and that returned a privacy error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The links at the end are broken; they're https instead of http, which causes a privacy error.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pppffffft. Good. Job. And sure, this is canon now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A delightfully spooky tall tale that maybe isn't so tall (because anything can and does happen in the weird west!), and I really love how you re-imagined all those SCP characters for the Aces & Eights canon.

Only just occurred to me after reading this, but perhaps the Nameless Saloon once had a different name.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Alright, so! This was a pinch hit gift made for Cosmic Catacalysm, for the 2018 (7th Annual) SCP Art Exchange! This is basically just a story about SCP-4466 put in the Aces & Eights canon. I didn't have much to go off of, so I basically just did what first came to mind and plugged Cosmic in as the main character. I think it turned out pretty well!

Thing is, transferring SCP-4466 was not as easy as I thought. I thought, you know, that something equine with a character named literally The Cowboy would be super easy to transfer over to the wild west canon. But, plenty of aspects of the skip were untransferrable. For example!

  • Part of The Cowboy's creep was that he was behind a screen. You could only observe him, from recorded episodes. No possible interaction.
  • The Cowboy could have possibly been a victim(?) of SCP-4466. Here he is clearly a dominant force, even if he is some form of "cursed".
  • The dichotomy between SCP-4466 and horses was much harder to cleverly highlight. In fact, I barely did so at all, and they come off as just strange horse-like creatures (which, hey, is pretty accurate anyways).
  • Some of the themes were far less subtle (see: salt).

Anyways, this version of The Cowboy and his steeds is not bad in my opinion. In fact, I loved writing it. It was very fun to write. But, it is very different from the original SCP-4466. A totally different vibe, honestly. Which is alright. But I also don't want people to think that The Cowboy here is the exact same character as The Cowboy in SCP-4466. Same with the steeds.

Anyways, that's about it. Thanks for reading! Loved sneaking in all those cameos to my own stuff, heheheh.
